channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: NEM Insurance and Fidelity Bank Highlighted as Strong Investment Options
item.description: The article discusses the Nigerian stock market's recent performance, noting a 1.2% decline over three weeks due to the T+1 settlement cycle introduced in June. The Banking and Insurance Indices were ...

item.description: The funeral procession for Iran’s assassinated supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei began in Tehran, accompanied by strict security protocols to prevent chaos reminiscent of his predecessor’s 1989 fu...

item.title: Regional Parks Workers in Metro Vancouver Initiate Strike Over Contract Disputes
item.description: The Greater Vancouver Regional District Employees’ Union has announced an indefinite strike by workers responsible for maintaining 25 regional parks, including Grouse Mountain and Capilano Regional Pa...

item.title: Tech Leaders Reassess AI's Impact on Employment: Optimism Over Job Losses
item.description: The article explores a significant shift in perspective among major technology companies regarding the impact of artificial intelligence (AI) on employment. Initially, many business leaders predicted ...

item.title: Iran's new supreme leader skips father's funeral amid political tensions
item.description: The article details the absence of Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, from the funeral of his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, which drew thousands of mourners in Tehran. Senior Iranian offic...

item.title: Super Typhoon Bavi Causes Significant Damage to US Pacific Islands
item.description: Super Typhoon Bavi, with winds up to 290 km/h, devastated the US Pacific territories of Northern Marianas and Guam on July 6, 2026. The storm's eye fully engulfed Rota, leaving residents stranded and ...

item.title: Kenya Police Summon MP Zaheer Jhanda to Assist in Kisii Convoy Attack Probe
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) in Kenya has summoned Nyaribari Chache Member of Parliament Zaheer Jhanda to provide information regarding the violent attacks on a Linda Mwananchi con...

item.title: Vice President Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial Begins
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte commenced on July 6, 2026, marking a significant political development in the Philippines. The live updates provided detailed coverage of the proce...

item.title: Google Executive Successfully Ports Classic RTS Game to iPhone and Mac
item.description: A Google lead product executive has successfully ported the 2003 PC real-time strategy game Command & Conquer Generals: Zero Hour to iPhone and iPad devices using a combination of Fable 5 and AI tools...

item.title: Parliament's Monsoon Session to Address Key Reforms and Legislative Debates
item.description: The Monsoon Session of Parliament will be held from July 20 to August 13, as announced by Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju. President Droupadi Murmu has approved the session, which aims to ...

item.title: Toronto Health Officials Warn of Measles Exposure at Scarborough Hospital and Clinic
item.description: Toronto Public Health has issued a warning about potential measles exposure at specific locations within the Scarborough Health Network. The exposure is linked to a travel-related measles case, though...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte to Appear via Counsel in Impeachment Trial
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte has opted to appear before the Senate impeachment court through her legal counsel rather than attend the formal opening of her impeachment trial. Her lawyer, Atty. Michael ...

item.title: Nairobi Lawyer Killed in Athi River Incident
item.description: A 30-year-old Nairobi-based lawyer, Edward Kariuki Muthee, was found dead outside an Athi River residential estate on July 5, 2026. The incident occurred after a security guard noticed his body motion...

item.title: Canada's procurement reforms critical for infrastructure and defense goals
item.description: The article emphasizes the urgent need for Canada to modernize its procurement processes to address a national infrastructure deficit estimated between $150 billion and $1 trillion. With increased def...

item.title: Challenges Facing the KwaZulu-Natal Textile Industry
item.description: The textile industry in KwaZulu-Natal is facing significant challenges due to compliance issues, retailer actions, and regulatory scrutiny. Raids on clothing manufacturing units (CMTs) in February 202...

item.title: The EU's Evolution Post-Brexit: A New Era of Integration and Geopolitical Shifts
item.description: The article examines the transformation of the European Union since the UK's Brexit exit, highlighting its shift toward greater fiscal integration, industrial policy, and geopolitical assertiveness. K...

item.title: Nigeria's Fuel Subsidy Removal and Its Impact on Public Finances
item.description: The article discusses Nigeria's decision to remove fuel subsidies in 2023, which aimed to address a significant fiscal burden. While the policy succeeded in eliminating a costly distortionary expendit...

item.title: China Conducts Submarine-Launched Ballistic Missile Test Amid Regional Concerns
item.description: China conducted a rare test of a submarine-launched ballistic missile (SLBM) in the Pacific Ocean on July 6, 2026, prompting criticism from New Zealand and Australia. The test, carried out by a Type 0...

item.title: Albanese's podcast controversy highlights ongoing political sexism debates
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese faced backlash after a casual podcast interview with comedian Nikki Osborne, where he jokingly referenced Kylie Minogue in a 'shag, marry, date' game. The remark, descr...

item.title: PAF Officer Sacrifices Life to Save Woman from Abduction, PM and President Pay Tribute
item.description: Pakistan Air Force (PAF) Group Captain Asim Tariq lost his life while attempting to rescue a woman from an alleged abduction attempt in Islamabad. Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and President Asif Ali ...
